channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.ign.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.ign.com

item.title: Holy Moly, Crusader Kings 3 Will Let You Play as a Pope
item.content:encoded:
Crusader Kings 3 is getting a new core expansion, By God Alone, and it's going to add playable popes to the Paradox strategy game alongside other new gameplay mechanics to let players influence the church.

"For the first time in the Crusader Kings franchise, we're also introducing playable theocracies, allowing you to rise to the power of the Pope himself," said Mikael Andersson, lead designer on the expansion. "By God Alone extends your options of how to engage with your Christian faith and the church behind it. It gives you more strategic options for how to deal with this institution, as well as dealing with other rulers of the same faith as you."

By God Alone will arrive in Q3 of this year as a paid expansion, so you've plenty of time to figure out how to wield the word of god to your strategic advantage.

"In this core expansion, we're fleshing out the Christian church to make the institution itself feel like a living entity," Andersson said during a video reveal for the expansion. "We're revising the way the church collects taxes to make it more obvious how taxation from temple holdings are being collected and allow the player to direct it to some extent. During the time span of Crusader Kings 3, the Christian faith went through a lot of upheaval. To represent this, we're introducing rites, local variations of practice that the player can interact with."

The reveal video also talked about another core expansion, Silk & Silver, which will allow players to live that Middle Ages merchant life, establishing trade routes and undercutting their business rivals. There's also a free update dropping today, which adds "age-related ailments" for older characters and a new system for personal character-bound Stories within the game.

Crusader Kings 3 is a strategy game where you play through whole dynasties with plenty of drama, marriages, and feuds. Both expansions will add yet more depth to an already rich set of mechanics in the game that IGN's review called "a new king of historical strategy" when it was released in 2022.

Rachel Weber is the Head of Editorial Development at IGN and an elder millennial. She's been a professional nerd since 2006 when she got her start on Official PlayStation Magazine in the UK, and has since worked for GamesIndustry.Biz, Rolling Stone and GamesRadar. She loves horror, horror movies, horror games, Red Dead Redemption 2, and her Love and Deepspace boyfriends.

Recommends

In This Article

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokémon TCG Has Two New Ascended Heroes Releases Dropping This Month
item.content:encoded:
April 2026 is turning out to be a mega month in the Pokémon Trading Card Game calendar. Players will be able to get their hands on two new releases from the Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes expansion when they drop on April 24, 2026. Whether you prefer volume pack opening, or love collecting rare promos, there’s something for everyone with these two new products.

The first release is a brand new Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle. If you’ve been struggling to get your hands on Pokémon’s recent Trading Card Game set, this could be your lucky day!

Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution - Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle

Each Booster Bundle comes with six booster packs, each including 10 cards, 1 Basic Energy, and 1 Pokémon TCG Live code card. That’s right - Trainers' Pokémon and Stellar Tera Pokémon ex join returning and newly discovered Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, including new illustration rare and special rare cards to collect.

If you want to pick up the Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle on 24 April 2026, you'd best be quick. Ascended Heroes has been flying off the shelves. Your best bet is always the official Pokémon Center for MSRP - $23.94.

Preorders aren't currently live here, so keep checking back. You can also find pre-order listings at reputable third-party reseller TCGPlayer, but the cheapest is currently $102.95 + $29.99 shipping.

The second new Ascended Heroes product, later this month, is technically three products. You can get three new Mega ex Boxes, each focusing on Pokémon Legends: Z-A’s partner Pokémon. Who will you choose? Each collection features a stunning foil promo card starring one of three new Mega Evolutions of Pokémon Legends: Z-A’s Partner Pokémon - Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, or Mega Feraligatr ex.

Ascended Heroes - Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, and Mega Feraligatr ex Boxes

Visually, these cards are a treat for long-time collectors. The vibrant, jagged energy lines bouncing out of the frame remind me of the iconic M Pokémon-EX from the XY Series, or the more recent Mega Evolution Special Illustration Rares designed by Saboteri. Whether you’re currently exploring the sectors of Lumiose City in Pokémon Legends: Z-A or you’re finally grinding out the post-game Battle Royale to pick up Mega Floette for the jump to Pokémon Champions, these promos are gorgeous additions to any binder.

Beyond their looks, Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, and Mega Feraligatr ex all pack a punch. Each boasts devastating attacks designed to work perfectly with existing cards from their respective evolution chains. Blossoming with new power, Mega Meganium ex presents your opponent’s Pokémon with a Giant Bouquet filled with massive damage. Surrounded by flames, Mega Emboar ex lets loose a Crimson Blast to devastate your opponent. And with gnashing teeth and claws, Mega Feraligatr ex targets your opponent’s weakened Pokémon with a Mortal Crunch.

You’ll also get one oversize lenticular promo card featuring Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, or Mega Feraligatr ex. While fan opinion on these oversized cards is split, seeing as you’d need the biggest binder known to man to fit one, it’s still a nice to have. The real prize is the four Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes booster packs inside.

While you can wait for pre-orders to go live at the Pokemon Center, Amazon has just restocked pre-orders for these collections at $36.99. It’s worth noting you can’t pick your Partner Pokémon here - you’ll receive, at random, one Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Ascended Heroes ex box featuring either Mega Meganium ex, Mega Emboar ex, or Mega Feraligatr ex. Individual selection is not available.

While smaller additions to the Pokémon Trading Card Game 2026 release calendar, these two launches are set to curb the out of control hype for Ascended Heroes. Will you be picking up one of these expansions?

Sara Heritage is a journalist and freelance contributor, with 8 years of experience in gaming journalism. When she's not gaming, you’ll probably find her building yet another LEGO set I absolutely did not need (yes, I’ve got thoughts on the Pokémon LEGO).



https://www.ign.com/articles/pokemon-tcg-ascended-heroes-new-releases-april-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The CyberPowerPC RTX 5070 Gaming PC With 32GB of DDR5 RAM Drops to $1399, Includes Pragmata
item.content:encoded:
The CyberPowerPC RTX 5070 Gaming PC With 32GB of DDR5 RAM Drops to $1399, Includes Pragmata

One of the least expensive RTX 5070 prebuilts I've seen this year, and now includes a hot new PC game.

Despite the rising prices of graphics cards and DDR5 RAM, there are still good deals to be found on prebuilt gaming PCs. B&H Photo is offering one such excellent deal this week. The CyberPowerPC Gamer Supreme Liquid gaming PC, featuring a GeForce RTX 5070 GPU, is on sale for just $1399 with free shipping after a $300 off instant discount. This is a well-equipped prebuilt that pairs the GPU with an AMD Ryzen 7 processor and a generous amount of RAM and storage.

Save $300 Off the CyperPowerPC RTX 5070 Gaming PC

CyberPowerPC Gamer Supreme AMD Ryzen 7 8700F GeForce RTX 5070 Gaming PC (32GB/2TB)

The CyberPowerPC Liquid Gamer Supreme gaming PC is equipped with a liquid-cooled AMD Ryzen 7 8700F CPU, GeForce RTX 5070 12GB GPU, 32GB of DDR5-6000MHz RAM, and a 2TB M.2 SSD. The AMD Ryzen 7 8700F CPU is an 8-core 16-thread processor with a max boost clock of 5GHz. It's a solid general-purpose Zen 4 CPU that combines a high clock speed and plenty of cores for unhindered gaming performance. Zen 4 CPUs is also compatible with newer DDR5 memory.

The GeForce RTX 5070 GPU is an excellent choice for 1080p or 1440p gaming

The GeForce RTX 5070 GPU is an excellent choice for 1080p or 1440p gaming. Compared to the previous generation GPUs, the RTX 5070 offers a slight performance improvement over the RTX 4070 Super, which was and still is an excellent GPU. The fps gain is greater in games that support DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generation. Check out our RTX 5070 review.

Pragmata makes full use of all of Nvidia's most recent tech, including DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generating, path tracing, ray reconstruction, and super resolution. It will run at 60fps and beyond on an RTX 5070 at 1440p, even without DLSS.

Eric Song is the IGN commerce manager in charge of finding the best gaming and tech deals every day. When Eric isn't hunting for deals for other people at work, he's hunting for deals for himself during his free time.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/cyberpowerpc-rtx-5070-gaming-pc-deal-includes-pragmata
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Take-Two Boss Strauss Zelnick Suggests AI Could Take Elon Musk's Job
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick has suggested that Elon Musk might want to watch out for artificial intelligence taking his job after the Tesla boss proposed that Grand Theft Auto 6 could be generated by AI.

The gaming bigwig, who oversees major subsidiaries within Take-Two like 2K and Rockstar Games, shared his insight into the rapid advancements in technology during a conversation at Semafor World Economy 2026. It was a quick conversation that touched on a number of topics, but it was the issue of AI that probably received the most attention.

After saying he feels "people spend too much time talking about the 'woe is me' risk" related to the tech despite the fact that it “can be used for evil purposes," Zelnick brought up Musk and his relationship to AI, unprompted. While Zelnick is optimistic about how AI can be used as a tool for the average worker, he isn’t so sure the billionaire X/Twitter owner’s job is quite as safe.

"If AI were going to get rid of employment, the richest man on Earth, Elon Musk, knows a little something about AI, last time I checked," the Take-Two head said. "He has unlimited financial resources, and he has unlimited human resources, and he has, apparently, an unlimited number of ideas. He knows his way around AI.

"The man works 20 hours a day. If AI were going to take anyone’s job, wouldn’t it take his job? The richest guy on Earth, wouldn’t that be job number one for AI to take? Why is he so busy? By the way, why am I working harder than ever despite the fact that I’ve totally accepted AI into every part of my life?"

Zelnick’s comments follow an exchange that happened on Musk’s social media platform earlier this year, where he said, "yeah," in response to a post that said, "there is definitely a non-zero chance we get ‘generate your own GTA 6 in a few minutes’ before GTA 6." Rockstar’s latest Grand Theft Auto game, which has been in development for around six years and is poised to be one of the biggest video game launches ever, is currently set with a release date of November 19, 2026.

You won’t even have to ask. AI will figure what video game you’d like best.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) January 14, 2026

Zelnick previously offered a more indirect response to Musk in March, saying at the time that the idea AI could generate quality video games "laughable." He added at the time, "These tools may help you create assets, but that won’t help you create hits."

Zelnick’s thoughts on Musk and his relationship with AI came to an end with a joke about how the Tesla billionaire could be "a simulation." As the audience laughed, he said, "In fairness, if you were going to choose a person who were a simulation, he would be my number one choice."

GTA 6 is currently expected to launch later this year. While we wait, you can read about the fake Grand Theft Auto leaks that started gaining traction on social media late last year.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/take-two-boss-strauss-zelnick-suggests-ai-could-take-elon-musks-job
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
